untranslatable_reason: Point-and-figure signals require tracking multi-bar column formations (consecutive Xs for upswings, consecutive Os for downswings) and comparing column heights across previous columns. Standard OHLCV indicators cannot replicate the box-by-box accumulation logic or multi-column comparisons inherent to P&F methodology. The signal requires columnar state machine logic not expressible with allowed indicators.
translation_notes: KAUFMAN_DRY_5 is a pure point-and-figure pattern signal. Attempted approximation uses prev_high/prev_low crosses as a simplistic reversal proxy, but this misses the core P&F mechanics: (1) box-size normalization, (2) column accumulation, (3) comparing current column peak/trough to previous column peak/trough. A proper implementation requires custom P&F indicator calculation external to this framework.
